name: Improve solver3 construction relative broad-suite score

description: Baseline for softened relative broad-suite construction metric
Repeat baseline measurement for relative metric noise calibration
Increase outside-region move penalty under relative metric
Tried stronger selected-region move bonus with outside penalty; worsened relative score despite better 169x13 constrained

asi.hypothesis: Increasing the within-selected-region move bonus might preserve selected-template basin locality while the existing outside-region penalty prevents disruptive pulls.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary broad_relative_score regressed versus current best; the improvement on score_sgp_169x13x14_constrained was outweighed by worse Sailing, large immovable, clique, 49x7 constrained, and a zero-regression penalty.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not simply increase selected-region move bonus globally. Prefer more targeted merge scoring that reduces outside pulls without over-prioritizing any selected-region move.

description: Tried lower scaffold-disruption weight under relative metric; near best but still worse and adds zero-regression

asi.hypothesis: Under the relative metric, a lower scaffold-disruption penalty may let the oracle improve large cases such as Sailing enough to beat the old log-optimized 3x setting.
asi.rollback_reason: The relative mean improved, but one zero-baseline regression penalty made the primary metric slightly worse than current best; several constrained SGP sentinels also regressed.
asi.next_action_hint: A more selective zero-regression guard or template choice may be needed; avoid globally lowering disruption weight without protecting zero-baseline solved cases.

description: Tried 2.5x scaffold-disruption weight under relative metric; regressed broadly

asi.hypothesis: A moderate scaffold-disruption weight may capture part of the 2x large-case benefit while avoiding the zero-regression/balance issues.
asi.rollback_reason: 2.5x did not preserve the 2x benefits and regressed the relative mean plus zero-regression guard; Sailing, large immovable, Google CP, and constrained SGP signals worsened.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not tune disruption weight further in this simple scalar form unless paired with a principled zero-regression or candidate-risk mechanism.

description: Tried stronger current-group keep bonus with outside penalty; regressed relative score

asi.hypothesis: A stronger bonus for keeping a person in its scaffold group when that group is also the oracle target could preserve basins while still accepting clear oracle-aligned placements.
asi.rollback_reason: The primary relative metric worsened; zero-regression penalty appeared and large immovable / synthetic / 49x7 constrained sentinels regressed despite some constrained 169x13 improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not globally raise keep_bonus. If preserving current groups is needed, make it conditional on objective/risk signals rather than a larger blanket constant.

description: Tried stronger outside-region move penalty 3x; over-constrained merge and regressed relative score

asi.hypothesis: A stronger outside-region move penalty might further avoid basin-hostile pulls while preserving the useful 2x direction.
asi.rollback_reason: 3x over-constrained the merge: relative mean worsened, zero-regression penalty returned, and large/SGP constrained sentinels regressed versus the 2x keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep outside-region penalty at 2.0; do not tune it upward as a scalar.

description: Revert outside-region penalty to 1.0 under relative metric

asi.hypothesis: Under the relative metric, the original outside-region penalty may be preferable to the old kept 2.0 value because large real-world improvements now matter more.
asi.learned: Reverting outside-region penalty to 1.0 improved the primary relative metric below 1.0 with no zero-regression penalty, mainly through a much better Sailing score while preserving no-template sentinels.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop scalar-only tuning and pursue larger heuristic changes such as oracle-guided baseline fill or multi-template/risk-aware candidate choice.

description: Tried surgical oracle merge instead of clearing template groups; regressed relative score

asi.hypothesis: A surgical merge that moves accepted projected people and displaces only target-group occupants as needed should preserve more of the baseline search basin than clearing every selected template group.
asi.rollback_reason: The structural change did preserve Sailing reasonably but regressed the primary relative metric through zero-regression penalty and worse large constrained SGP, especially 169x13x14 constrained.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not replace clear-and-repair with this naive surgical merge. If revisiting surgical merge, add stronger capacity/zero-regression risk selection or candidate choice first.

description: Tried projection-aware top-3 oracle template selection; caused two benchmark failures

asi.hypothesis: Choosing among the top three templates using projection-aware score should avoid bad top-ranked templates without score-gating final acceptance.
asi.rollback_reason: The naive projection-aware top-3 selector caused two benchmark case failures (large gender immovable and transfer attribute sentinels became failure placeholders), so the candidate selector was not strict/safe enough.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not evaluate/choose multiple templates solely by projection score. A future multi-template path needs hard feasibility/risk prechecks or failure isolation without masking strict merge errors.

description: Return optimal warmup scaffold before oracle merge

asi.hypothesis: If the full-objective warmup scaffold already has zero score, oracle injection can only preserve or worsen it, so returning the scaffold before template generation is a generic optimal-incumbent guard rather than a fallback.
asi.learned: The zero-score scaffold guard slightly improved the relative metric and kept zero-regression count at 0. It mainly protects solved cases while preserving useful oracle behavior elsewhere.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue structural work, but keep this optimal-scaffold guard. Next focus on oracle-guided fill/risk selection for nonzero scaffolds.

description: Tried accepting oracle moves only when target beats current group; damaged SGP structure badly

asi.hypothesis: Accepted oracle placements should be advisory: only move a person if the oracle target scores better than staying in its current scaffold group under local pair/placement signals.
asi.rollback_reason: The local stay-vs-target filter broke the pure/SGP oracle structure badly, introducing multiple zero-baseline regressions and a huge 169x13 constrained regression.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not filter individual oracle moves independently against current-group score; preserving coherent whole-template structure matters for SGP cases.

description: Tried two-second warmup scaffold budget; caused a benchmark failure despite better relative mean

asi.hypothesis: A longer warmup search may produce a better full-objective scaffold before oracle injection, improving final construction+search quality despite less search time after construction.
asi.rollback_reason: The two-second warmup improved the non-penalized relative mean but introduced one benchmark failure and much higher construction time, making the primary metric catastrophic.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not globally increase warmup to 2s. A future adaptive warmup needs feasibility safeguards and should not consume budget blindly.

description: Repeat current zero-score scaffold guard line under relative metric

asi.hypothesis: Repeat the current kept structural line to estimate noise and confirm the zero-score scaffold guard remains beneficial under the relative metric.
asi.learned: The same code can vary materially, but this repeat stayed below 1.0 with no failures or zero-regressions; the guard remains plausible and noise is large enough that future keeps should preferably be structural and robust across sentinel tradeoffs.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with structural changes, not scalar tuning; compare sentinel mix carefully because Sailing and SGP constrained scores are noisy.

description: Tried cohort-aware displaced repair scoring in oracle merge

asi.hypothesis: When oracle merge displaces people, repair should fill slots using scaffold cohort agreement in addition to placement frequency so the incumbent stays search-basin-friendly rather than arbitrary.
asi.rollback_reason: Cohort-aware repair worsened the primary metric, especially the Sailing sentinel, while not improving constrained SGP enough to offset it.
asi.next_action_hint: Repair should not over-preserve scaffold cohorts after oracle placement; focus instead on pre-merge/fill ordering or safer template candidate choice.

description: Tried requiring dummy-free oracle templates when available

asi.hypothesis: Prefer fully occupied oracle templates over templates with dummy oracle people when both exist, reducing artificial contacts and projection ambiguity without case-specific logic.
asi.rollback_reason: The dummy-free filter caused one benchmark failure (transfer attribute sentinel placeholder) and worsened constrained SGP balance despite improving Sailing in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not globally filter to dummy-free templates. Candidate selection needs feasibility/risk scoring that accounts for omitted people and constraints, not a hard dummy rule.

description: Tried prioritizing selected-region people before outside moves during oracle merge

asi.hypothesis: Accepted oracle targets should fill from the selected template region before pulling people from outside groups, reducing basin-disruptive outside moves while preserving coherent group-level oracle structure.
asi.rollback_reason: The result was near current best but did not improve the primary metric over the latest kept repeat; it also left several sentinels mixed, so the structural rule is not clearly robust.
asi.next_action_hint: Selected-region priority is promising but noisy; revisit only with stronger candidate-risk diagnostics or a fuller fill strategy.

description: Tried projecting only high-attendance people into oracle template

asi.hypothesis: The pure-contact oracle should be projected mainly onto high-attendance people so oracle-local identities represent stable participants rather than sparse attendees that create dummy-like holes across sessions.
asi.rollback_reason: High-attendance filtering improved some large/partial signals but worsened the primary metric, especially constrained SGP 49x7 and 169x13 sentinels.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not hard-filter low-attendance projectable people. Attendance should influence scoring, not eligibility, unless paired with candidate dimensions.

description: Tried skipping oracle when warmup scaffold has zero repeat penalty

asi.hypothesis: If the warmup scaffold already has zero raw repeat penalty and there is no maximize-unique-contacts objective, the repeat oracle has no repeat-score upside and should not disturb constraint/attribute structure.
asi.rollback_reason: The repeat-optimal scaffold guard was too broad and worsened the primary metric, likely skipping useful oracle geometry for search even when raw repeat penalty was zero at construction time.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep only the stricter total-score-zero guard. Repeat-component-only bypasses need more evidence and perhaps unique-contact/search-basin diagnostics.

description: Tried two-second warmup only when construction budget is at least four seconds

asi.hypothesis: The earlier 2s warmup improved relative mean but failed a small-budget constrained SGP case, so applying 2s only when construction budget is at least four seconds might capture scaffold-quality gains safely.
asi.rollback_reason: Budget-guarded 2s warmup removed the failure but worsened the primary metric and construction time, especially Sailing.
asi.next_action_hint: Longer warmup is not a simple win. Focus on merge/projection structure rather than spending more construction time globally.

description: Tried oracle-seeded baseline refill instead of direct scaffold overwrite merge

asi.hypothesis: Instead of destructively overwriting the scaffold, seed an otherwise empty schedule with frozen anchors plus projected oracle placements and let the legacy baseline fill the remaining movable slots to recover its search-friendly basin behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Oracle-seeded baseline refill did not improve the primary metric; it improved runtime and kept failures at zero but hurt synthetic/immovable sentinels and was weaker than the kept direct merge line.
asi.next_action_hint: A full oracle-guided fill needs objective-aware placement, not just baseline random fill after oracle seeding. Reuse of baseline alone is insufficient.

description: Tried scaffold fallback backfill for oracle template holes during merge

asi.hypothesis: When projected oracle groups have dummy/unmapped holes, fill remaining selected-region capacity with the original scaffold occupants before repair so the merge is less destructive without blocking oracle candidates.
asi.rollback_reason: The fallback backfill worsened the primary metric and constrained SGP 49x7 sentinel despite improving some individual cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Simple scaffold backfill can over-preserve local scaffold structure and weaken oracle geometry; future fill work needs objective-aware assignment rather than holes-only backfill.

description: Tried promoting lowest-risk candidate within top-three template frontier

asi.hypothesis: Within the leading estimated-score frontier, choose the structurally lowest-disruption oracle template to reduce basin damage without projection-score gating or case-specific logic.
asi.rollback_reason: Lowest-risk top-three promotion was much worse, particularly Sailing and constrained SGP 169x13, showing that the estimated-score ordering carries important contact-opportunity information that raw disruption risk cannot replace.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not select by disruption risk alone. Multi-template selection needs richer feasibility/contact tradeoff rather than a simple top-frontier risk promotion.

description: Tried one extra projection assignment/alignment convergence pass

asi.hypothesis: Projection currently aligns groups after the final person assignment without re-solving people for that final group map, so one extra assignment/alignment convergence pass should improve oracle-to-scaffold consistency.
asi.rollback_reason: The extra convergence pass improved some sentinels such as Sailing and transfer but worsened the primary metric through synthetic, large immovable, Google, and 49x7 constrained regressions.
asi.next_action_hint: Projection convergence alone changes tradeoffs but is not enough; if revisited, pair it with candidate/fill logic that protects synthetic and constrained SGP cases.

description: Use batch assignment for displaced oracle merge repair

asi.hypothesis: Greedy one-by-one repair after oracle merge is order-dependent; assigning all displaced people to open slots in one deterministic max-weight batch should preserve the same oracle placements while reducing arbitrary collateral damage.
asi.learned: Batch repair improved the primary metric to 0.97238 with no failures or zero regressions. It kept transfer and 49x7 gains, improved large immovable to baseline, but slightly worsened Google and 32x8x20 constrained.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep batch repair. Next experiments can refine the batch repair score or add a modest generic cohesion term, but avoid over-preserving scaffold cohorts based on prior negative repair experiment.

description: Tried adding modest scaffold cohort pressure to batch repair assignment

asi.hypothesis: Batch repair can remain global while modestly preferring open slots whose current members match the displaced person's scaffold cohort, reducing arbitrary repairs without the greedy cohort-overpreservation failure.
asi.rollback_reason: Even a 0.25 cohort-pressure term badly worsened Sailing, large immovable, Google, and constrained SGP sentinels; preserving scaffold cohorts in repair is basin-hostile here.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep batch assignment with placement/preferred-group score only. Avoid adding scaffold cohort pressure to repair.

description: Tried weaker preferred-group prior in batch repair assignment

asi.hypothesis: Batch repair may be over-anchored to each displaced person's original selected group, so reducing the preferred-group prior from 2 to 1 could let the assignment use open capacity more flexibly.
asi.rollback_reason: Weakening the preferred-group prior badly worsened the primary metric and several sentinels; the original-group prior appears important for search-basin stability after batch repair.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the preferred-group repair prior at 2.0. Avoid loosening repair anchoring unless another structural mechanism compensates.

description: Tried stronger preferred-group prior in batch repair assignment

asi.hypothesis: Since weakening the preferred-group repair prior was harmful, a stronger original-group anchor might further stabilize search basins after batch repair.
asi.rollback_reason: Increasing the preferred-group prior to 3.0 also worsened the metric and sentinels; the kept value 2.0 appears to balance repair anchoring and flexibility best among tested values.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not continue scalar tuning of repair_group_score; keep 2.0 and look for structural improvements elsewhere.

description: Tried selected-region target priority on top of batch repair

asi.hypothesis: The earlier selected-region priority nearly improved the metric with greedy repair; combining it with the kept batch repair might preserve its basin benefits while reducing collateral repair damage.
asi.rollback_reason: With batch repair, selected-region priority became much worse, especially large immovable and Google sentinels; the kept batch repair does not need this hard regional ordering.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not combine hard selected-region target priority with batch repair. Keep target selection score-based.

description: Retried available-capacity-only oracle template sizing with batch repair

asi.hypothesis: Template group size/count should be based on non-frozen available capacity rather than full group capacity, reducing overambitious oracle blocks around fixed anchors now that repair is batch-assigned.
asi.rollback_reason: Available-capacity template sizing remained harmful and caused a constrained 169x13 SGP failure plus severe SGP regressions.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revisit available-capacity-only template sizing; frozen anchors need to remain part of the projected oracle structure rather than simply shrinking capacity.

description: Tried stronger absent-session penalty in oracle person projection

asi.hypothesis: Attendance should influence projection softly rather than as the previously failed hard high-attendance filter, so a stronger absent-session penalty may reduce unstable partial-attendance oracle identities.
asi.rollback_reason: Increasing absent-session penalty to 0.5 worsened the primary metric, especially Sailing, transfer, and large immovable sentinels; soft attendance pressure also disrupts useful projection choices.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep absent-session penalty at 0.20. Do not continue attendance scalar tuning without a broader candidate/fill redesign.

description: Tried extra projection convergence pass with batch repair

asi.hypothesis: The previous projection convergence pass improved Sailing/transfer before batch repair; combining it with batch displaced repair might keep those gains while reducing collateral merge damage.
asi.rollback_reason: With batch repair, the extra projection pass worsened the primary metric, especially Google and constrained SGP 169x13, despite improving synthetic and large immovable.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add the extra projection convergence pass on the batch-repair line. Projection changes need a different safeguard.

description: Tried removing scaffold pair-pressure from oracle target acceptance

asi.hypothesis: Oracle target acceptance may over-preserve scaffold pair cohorts; removing target-group pair pressure should let projected oracle structure drive target selection while batch repair preserves feasibility.
asi.rollback_reason: Removing pair pressure improved Sailing strongly but worsened the primary metric through transfer, Google, 49x7, and 169x13 constrained regressions.
asi.next_action_hint: Target pair pressure is important for broad balance. A future variant could make this structural signal conditional on template risk, but do not simply remove it.

description: Tried half-weight scaffold pair-pressure in oracle target acceptance

asi.hypothesis: Removing target pair pressure improved Sailing but hurt broad balance, so half-weight pair pressure might preserve some Sailing gains while retaining enough scaffold signal for constrained/transfer cases.
asi.rollback_reason: Half-weight pair pressure remained worse than kept batch repair: transfer stayed good but Google, 49x7, and 169x13 constrained regressions dominated.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep target pair pressure at full weight. Avoid scalar tuning of this term unless paired with structural risk diagnostics.

description: Tried using baseline scaffold without warmup search

asi.hypothesis: The full-objective warmup may be consuming construction time and over-shaping the scaffold; using the baseline scaffold directly could preserve legacy basin behavior while leaving more time for normal search.
asi.rollback_reason: Skipping warmup caused a zero-baseline regression and worsened Sailing/overall metric despite much lower construction time.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the 1s warmup scaffold. Warmup is important for feasibility/zero guards and broad basin quality.

description: Tried anti-cohort pressure in batch repair assignment

asi.hypothesis: Positive scaffold cohort pressure in repair was harmful, so a modest anti-cohort term might increase repeat diversity while batch assignment still preserves preferred groups and feasibility.
asi.rollback_reason: Anti-cohort repair worsened the primary metric, especially Sailing, Google, and transfer, despite improving 49x7/169x13 constrained scores.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep repair assignment score simple: preferred group plus placement frequency only. Both positive and negative cohort terms are harmful.

description: Retried lower scaffold-disruption template penalty with batch repair

asi.hypothesis: Because batch repair reduces merge destructiveness, a slightly lower scaffold-disruption penalty could allow stronger oracle templates without the earlier repair damage.
asi.rollback_reason: Lowering disruption penalty to 2.5 worsened the primary metric, especially Sailing and transfer, even with batch repair.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep scaffold-disruption penalty at 3.0. Avoid more simple disruption scalar tuning.

description: Repeat kept batch-repair line to check noise

asi.hypothesis: Rerun the kept batch-repair code without code changes to estimate broad-suite noise and avoid over-interpreting one lucky measurement.
asi.rollback_reason: The repeat was substantially worse than the kept 0.972 run, showing significant benchmark/search variability; no code change to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat small improvements cautiously. Prefer structural changes with robust sentinel improvements or rerun near-threshold candidates before keeping.

description: Tried global slot assignment for oracle target acceptance

asi.hypothesis: Instead of accepting projected oracle people independently per target group, assign all projected candidates to selected-region capacity slots in one deterministic max-weight pass so overflow can use compatible neighboring selected groups and reduce repair damage.
asi.rollback_reason: Global cross-target acceptance destroyed coherent SGP structure, causing four zero-baseline regressions and huge constrained SGP regressions.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not let projected oracle people drift across target groups in acceptance. Preserve group-coherent oracle targets; improvements should stay in repair or candidate selection.

description: Tried oracle co-target pair pressure in target-local acceptance

asi.hypothesis: Ranking projected candidates within each oracle target by max(scaffold target pair pressure, oracle co-target pair pressure) might avoid stale scaffold-occupant bias without breaking target-local SGP coherence.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus the kept batch-repair line; Sailing and transfer remained worse and large constrained SGP 169x13x14 regressed materially despite no zero-baseline failures.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add oracle-cohort pressure to target acceptance. Target-local coherence alone is not enough; additional cohort pressure appears basin-hostile similar to repair-side cohort terms.

description: Tried available-capacity-first template slot selection

asi.hypothesis: Selecting template groups by non-frozen available capacity before nominal capacity might avoid overcommitting oracle blocks around fixed anchors while still using full-capacity template sizing.
asi.rollback_reason: The candidate was feasible but did not beat the kept batch-repair line; transfer and 49x7 constrained regressed, and Sailing was not enough to compensate.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not prioritize available capacity at slot-selection level in this simple form. Fixed anchors need projection/risk handling rather than changing group slot order globally.

description: Tried baseline-warmup consensus rigidity overlay

asi.hypothesis: Placements that agree between the cheap baseline fill and 1s full-objective warmup may be stable search-basin anchors, so use them as soft rigidity while keeping pair pressure/placement signals from the warmed scaffold.
asi.rollback_reason: Non-penalized relative mean was near the kept line but introduced a zero-baseline regression and worsened synthetic/large sentinels; consensus rigidity over-preserved incidental anchors.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add baseline/warmup consensus rigidity as a soft anchor in this simple form. If using multiple scaffolds, avoid rigidity-like preservation and use telemetry or fill-order signals instead.

description: Tried negative dummy assignment score in oracle projection

asi.hypothesis: Making dummy oracle-person assignments mildly negative should prefer weak real partial-attendance mappings over holes, reducing merge holes without the previously failed hard dummy-free template filter.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened and 49x7/169x13 constrained regressed; forcing extra real mappings seems to inject unstable people rather than useful contact structure.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep dummy assignment neutral. Dummy burden should be handled by candidate/fill strategy, not by pressuring projection to map weak real people.

description: Tried freedom-aware scaffold source before warmup search

asi.hypothesis: Using the repeat-aware freedom-aware constructor as the pre-warmup scaffold source might give solver3 search a better repeat structure before oracle projection while retaining warmup feasibility repair.
asi.rollback_reason: Freedom-aware scaffold source caused six benchmark failures/timeouts/placeholders despite a low non-penalized relative mean on surviving cases; it is not a safe universal scaffold source for oracle-guided construction.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep BaselineLegacy as the scaffold source before warmup. Do not replace it wholesale with freedom-aware construction; any reuse must be narrower and feasibility-safe.

description: Tried removing projection contact-signature anchoring

asi.hypothesis: Projection may over-anchor to scaffold cohorts through contact-signature pressure; removing that term while keeping target acceptance pair pressure could let oracle identity mapping be less basin-hostile.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remained worse than the kept line; large immovable, transfer, synthetic, and constrained SGP sentinels regressed, so projection contact signatures are protective for broad balance.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep CONTACT_SIGNATURE_WEIGHT at 0.10. Do not remove projection contact anchoring unless paired with a stronger fill/order redesign.

description: Tried stronger projection contact-signature anchoring

asi.hypothesis: Since removing projection contact signatures regressed broad balance, increasing contact-signature anchoring might better preserve constraint-scenario contact structure while target acceptance stays unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Stronger contact anchoring worsened the primary metric, especially Sailing, large immovable, transfer, and constrained SGP 49x7; the current 0.10 weight is a better balance than 0.0 or 0.20.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop scalar tuning of projection contact signature. Keep 0.10 and focus on fill/candidate structure instead.

name: Improve solver3 construction relative broad-suite score with flotilla sentinel

description: Baseline attempt failed because delegated autoresearch script was not executable

asi.hypothesis: Record new baseline for the updated construction broad suite including the landing flotilla sentinel.
asi.rollback_reason: No benchmark ran; root wrapper execs tools/autoresearch/solver3-construction/autoresearch.sh directly, but that delegated script lacks executable permission.
asi.next_action_hint: Set executable bits on delegated autoresearch scripts or change wrapper to invoke bash, commit the operational fix, then rerun baseline.

description: New baseline with sailing flotilla stress sentinel included

asi.hypothesis: Establish the required baseline for the construction broad suite after adding the landing-page sailing flotilla stress benchmark.
asi.learned: The updated 36-case baseline is dominated by the new flotilla sentinel failing construction budget; broad_relative_score is about 1001.137 with failure_count=1. The new sentinel records as score_sailing_flotilla_stress=1e9, while relative mean on successful baseline-weighted cases is about 1.078 and zero_regression_penalty about 0.059.
asi.next_action_hint: First priority is making stretch.sailing-flotilla-stress-test construct within the strict 9s construction budget without case-specific logic, then optimize final score tradeoffs across existing sentinels.

description: No-op hard-apart template skip did not compile

asi.hypothesis: Skip oracle solve/projection when every selected template session is hard-apart-preserving and every selected group is already full, because the merge policy cannot change such a scaffold.
asi.rollback_reason: Compile failed because OracleTemplateCandidate was only re-exported under cfg(test), and the helper's type import failed in non-test builds; follow-up should re-export the type for crate use or move the helper closer to template_candidates.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix the internal type visibility/import, then rerun the same experiment. The structural hypothesis remains valid and was not benchmarked.

description: Skip no-op oracle templates under full hard-apart merge policy

asi.hypothesis: When every selected oracle-template session has active hard-apart constraints and all selected groups are already full, the constructor's non-displacing hard-apart merge cannot change the scaffold, so solving/projecting an oracle is wasted and can exceed construction budget.
asi.learned: The generic no-op check removes the flotilla construction-budget failure and drops primary metric from the new 36-case baseline 1001.137 to 1.154. Flotilla now succeeds with score 834 instead of failure placeholder 1e9. Runtime fell from about 133s to 111s. Tradeoff: successful-case relative mean is still worse than historical best and zero-regression penalty remains; Google CP and transfer/large-immovable are weaker in this noisy run.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the no-op hard-apart skip. Next improve broad quality among successful cases, especially zero-regression guard plus Google CP / transfer / large immovable, while preserving flotilla success.

description: Repeat no-op hard-apart skip line for noise calibration

asi.hypothesis: Repeat the kept no-op hard-apart skip line to estimate noise after the huge failure-removal improvement.
asi.learned: No-code repeat remained failure-free and flotilla succeeded with an even better raw score 578, but broad_relative_score worsened to 1.196 because Sailing real and 32x8x20 constrained varied/regressed. Benchmark noise remains material, but the no-op skip's key qualitative property (no flotilla failure) held.
asi.rollback_reason: No code changes; repeat did not beat the kept run's primary metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat near-threshold improvements cautiously, but continue structural experiments. Preserve the no-op skip because it removes a deterministic construction-budget failure.

description: Select first oracle template that can affect the scaffold

asi.hypothesis: If the top-ranked oracle template is no-op under the hard-apart-safe merge policy, skip it and try the next generated candidate that can actually modify at least one selected session/group instead of immediately returning the scaffold.
asi.learned: Selecting the first change-capable template improved primary metric from kept 1.154 to 1.126 and reduced successful-case relative mean to 1.046 while keeping flotilla success. Construction time fell to about 25.4s total. The tradeoff is a larger zero-regression penalty and still-weak Google/transfer/large-immovable scores, but SGP constrained sentinels improved.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep change-capable candidate selection. Next address the remaining zero-regression on benchmark-very-large-constrained or the weak Google/transfer cases without losing flotilla success.

description: Tried longer pure-oracle local search budget

asi.hypothesis: The pure-structure oracle may be too shallow for medium unsupported SGP-like requests, so allowing more solver6 local-search iterations under the same one-second cap could improve oracle geometry and downstream construction quality.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed from 1.126 to 1.189. The longer oracle search improved flotilla raw score in this noisy run but worsened Sailing real and broad relative mean while keeping the zero-regression penalty.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep solver6 oracle stop conditions at 500/100. More oracle local search is not a broad win under the current projection/merge path; improve candidate/merge structure instead.

description: Tried fewer projection assignment iterations

asi.hypothesis: Projection may be over-iterating assignment/alignment; two assignment iterations could preserve most structure while saving construction time and giving search more budget.
asi.rollback_reason: Although the zero-regression guard improved to zero, primary metric regressed from 1.126 to 1.140 because successful-case relative mean worsened substantially, especially Sailing real, large immovable, Google, 49x7, and 169x13 constrained.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep PERSON_ASSIGNMENT_ITERATIONS at 3. If chasing zero-regression, do it with candidate/risk logic rather than reducing projection convergence globally.

name: Improve solver3 construction relative broad-suite score with weighted flotilla sentinel

description: Baseline with weighted sailing flotilla sentinel

asi.hypothesis: Rebaseline the construction broad-suite primary metric after assigning the first successful strict-budget flotilla sentinel score as a fixed weighted baseline.
asi.learned: With flotilla baseline fixed at 830, current code scores broad_relative_score=1.198681. Flotilla remains successful at 834, so the new weighted ratio is near 1.0; the main remaining penalty is one zero-regression plus weak successful-case ratios for Sailing real, Google CP, transfer, and large immovable.
asi.next_action_hint: Use 1.198681 as the clean baseline for this weighted-flotilla phase. Optimize broad relative score without losing flotilla success.

description: Use two projection assignment iterations under weighted flotilla metric

asi.hypothesis: After rebaselining the flotilla sentinel as weighted, reducing projection assignment/alignment iterations from 3 to 2 may be worthwhile because it trades a little projection convergence for lower construction overhead and better search budget while preserving flotilla success.
asi.learned: Under the weighted-flotilla metric, 2 projection iterations improved broad_relative_score from the clean baseline 1.1987 to 1.1262. The run preserved zero failures and improved relative mean, flotilla ratio, Sailing real, and constrained 49x7/169x13 sentinels, though one zero-regression remains and Google/transfer/large-immovable are still weak.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep 2 projection iterations for this weighted target, but consider repeating if future changes are near threshold because previous old-target run showed noise/tradeoff sensitivity.

description: Use one projection assignment iteration under weighted flotilla metric

asi.hypothesis: Projection convergence may still be overkill after no-op candidate filtering; a single assignment/alignment iteration could be enough and may save construction time for more search.
asi.learned: One projection iteration produced a marginal primary improvement over the two-iteration keep (1.12349 vs 1.12618) and cut zero_regression_penalty in this run, with good Sailing/flotilla/synthetic/large-immovable signals. It worsened 169x13 constrained and Google remained weak, so the win is small and noise-sensitive.
asi.next_action_hint: Repeat or be cautious with follow-up changes; if later runs show this was noise, return to two projection iterations.

description: Repeat one-iteration projection line under weighted metric

asi.hypothesis: Repeat the one-iteration projection line to check whether the marginal previous improvement was noise under the weighted-flotilla metric.
asi.learned: The repeat was much better at 1.08442, suggesting one projection iteration is not merely a one-run fluke. Large stochastic/search variation remains: runtime was longer and flotilla score improved to 610, while zero-regression penalty persisted.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep one projection iteration. Future near-threshold changes should still be confirmed because sentinel scores vary materially between no-code repeats.

description: Tried zero projection alignment warmup iterations

asi.hypothesis: Eliminate the iterative projection alignment loop entirely and rely on the initial group mapping plus final assignment/alignment, further reducing construction overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed to 1.1715. Although construction was faster, Sailing real and other successful-case ratios worsened enough to erase the benefit.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep one projection assignment/alignment iteration; zero is too little projection convergence.

description: Tried skipping oracle for attribute-balance scenarios

asi.hypothesis: Attribute-balance workloads may be harmed by injecting a pure-contact oracle, so returning the warmed constraint scaffold for any attribute-balance scenario could preserve constraint-friendly basins.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed catastrophically to 2.87, mainly due severe constrained SGP regressions including 169x13x14 constrained at 15011. The pure-contact oracle remains necessary even when attribute balance exists.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not skip oracle based solely on attribute-balance presence. Attribute-aware candidate/risk logic must be more selective.

description: Tried weaker projection contact-signature weight

asi.hypothesis: With only one projection iteration, the contact-signature term may be too strong; halving it could reduce over-anchoring while retaining some pair-pressure guidance.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus the best one-iteration repeat (1.114 vs 1.084), mostly through a larger zero-regression penalty and weaker flotilla/transfer despite better Google.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep CONTACT_SIGNATURE_WEIGHT at 0.10; do not weaken it in isolation under the one-iteration projection setting.

description: Double projection contact-signature weight

asi.hypothesis: With only one projection assignment/alignment iteration, stronger contact-signature anchoring may compensate for less convergence and preserve useful pair-pressure structure.
asi.learned: Doubling CONTACT_SIGNATURE_WEIGHT to 0.20 improved the weighted-flotilla primary metric to 1.044, with relative_score_mean below 1.0 and better Google/transfer/flotilla ratios. One zero-regression remains, but the broad successful-case mix improved enough to outweigh it.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep CONTACT_SIGNATURE_WEIGHT=0.20 under one projection iteration. Next test nearby stronger/lower values cautiously or target the remaining zero-regression.

description: Tried stronger projection contact-signature weight

asi.hypothesis: If 0.20 contact-signature anchoring helped with one projection iteration, a stronger 0.30 weight might further stabilize projected identities.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed to 1.133. 0.30 over-anchored projection, hurting Sailing real and large immovable despite decent Google and 49x7 signals.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep CONTACT_SIGNATURE_WEIGHT at 0.20; do not push this scalar higher without a different structural change.

description: Tried medium projection contact-signature weight

asi.hypothesis: A medium contact-signature weight between the original 0.10 and kept 0.20 might capture most of the stronger-anchor benefit while reducing zero-regression risk.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus 0.20. The relative mean was close to 1.0, but zero-regression penalty was higher and Google/transfer did not beat the kept 0.20 line.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep CONTACT_SIGNATURE_WEIGHT=0.20; 0.15 is not enough under one projection iteration.

description: Tried bounded small-oracle multistart selecting the better pure unique-contact oracle schedule

asi.hypothesis: A second bounded pure-structure oracle seed on <=64-person requests would improve construction basin quality enough to offset any cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus the current best observed weighted metric 1.044135489; Google-CP, transfer, synthetic, and constrained SGP metrics degraded despite flotilla staying good.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid more blind oracle multistart; investigate targeted generic construction/search-basin regressions or oracle-schedule selection criteria that include downstream constraint projection risk instead of pure contacts only.

description: Tried doubling oracle projection person-priority weight

asi.hypothesis: A stronger generic person-priority term would map high-pressure/flexible people into oracle positions more consistently and improve construction basins.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus 1.044135489; Google-CP and constrained 49x7 degraded strongly, and flotilla returned to baseline 830 despite some sailing-real gain.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not increase person-priority pressure; if revisiting projection ordering, use structural risk/attribute-aware mapping rather than a global scalar boost.

description: Tried removing oracle projection person-priority weight

asi.hypothesis: The projection assignment may be overusing person-priority noise; removing it could let contact/placement anchors dominate and improve broad basins.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus 1.044135489; Google-CP, transfer, sailing-real, synthetic, and large-gender metrics were worse despite some constrained SGP movement.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the small 0.01 person-priority tie-breaker; avoid more scalar priority tuning unless paired with a new structural projection criterion.

description: Repeated kept projection contact-anchor line without code changes

asi.hypothesis: A no-code repeat of the kept 0.20 contact-anchor line would estimate stochastic noise and possibly confirm the current best basin.
asi.rollback_reason: Repeat was worse than the best observed 1.044135489, so it does not replace the current best line.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat benchmark noise as material but not enough to explain the full gap; continue structural construction experiments and rerun only near-threshold improvements.

description: Tried adding scaffold pair-pressure anchoring to displaced repair assignment

asi.hypothesis: Displaced repair should preserve useful scaffold contact signatures by preferring open groups whose current members had pair pressure with the displaced person.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed substantially; sailing-real and large-gender worsened badly, indicating repair pair-pressure over-preserved scaffold contacts and damaged oracle diversity.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add scaffold pair pressure to displaced repair; if repair is revisited, use feasibility/attribute fill signals rather than contact preservation.

description: Tried attribute-balance delta scoring for displaced repair fill

asi.hypothesis: Displaced repair should use open slots to reduce attribute-balance penalty, improving attribute-heavy cases without affecting non-attribute cases.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed; sailing-real, flotilla, transfer, Google-CP, and synthetic worsened, showing this local fill signal disrupts broader construction basins.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add simple attribute-balance delta to displaced repair; attribute handling likely needs projection-level structural mapping, not late displaced fill scoring.

description: Tried bounded projection-level attribute-balance swap polishing

asi.hypothesis: A bounded projection-level swap polish for small attribute-balance templates would reduce attribute disruption before search and improve zero-regression/attribute cases.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus 1.044135489; relative mean and zero-regression penalty were both worse, with Google-CP and constrained SGP cases degraded.
asi.next_action_hint: Projection-level attribute swaps need a better combined objective or should be deprioritized; simple post-assignment swapping is not sufficient.

description: Tried stronger dummy-oracle penalty in template candidate scoring

asi.hypothesis: A stronger generic penalty for dummy oracle capacity would prefer cleaner templates without hard-filtering dummy candidates.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed; sailing-real, large-gender, Google-CP, flotilla, and zero-regression penalty worsened, so mild dummy avoidance in candidate scoring is still harmful.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid dummy-pressure scalar tuning; dummy burden needs a structural fill/projection change rather than candidate-score penalty.

description: Tried runtime-scaled early stop for construction warmup scaffold search

asi.hypothesis: Stopping the warmup scaffold search after runtime-scaled stagnation would save construction budget for final search without losing useful scaffold quality.
asi.rollback_reason: Although construction time dropped, primary metric regressed badly; scaffold quality loss outweighed extra search time, especially sailing-real, flotilla, Google-CP, and attribute cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the full 1s warmup; do not use generic early-stagnation warmup cutoff without a stronger quality-preservation signal.

description: Tried stronger projection placement-anchor weight

asi.hypothesis: Increasing projection placement anchoring would preserve useful scaffold structure while still allowing oracle contact geometry.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed; flotilla, Google-CP, zero-regression penalty, and constrained 169x13x14 worsened, suggesting over-anchoring harms oracle structure.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep placement anchor at 0.25; avoid more simple projection anchoring scalar tuning.

description: Tried retrying top oracle candidates when merge makes no placement changes

asi.hypothesis: Some templates may pass the pre-merge can-change check but ultimately make zero placements; retrying bounded top candidates only in that no-op case could find useful oracle changes.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed; flotilla and sailing-real worsened, indicating the retry path either changed candidate choice harmfully or added cost/noise without improving basins.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the existing pre-merge no-op skip only; do not add bounded post-merge no-op retries without telemetry showing actual zero-change merges.

description: Tried shorter pure-structure oracle local search budget

asi.hypothesis: A shorter solver6 pure-structure oracle search might preserve enough structure while reducing oracle cost and leaving more wall time for solver3 search.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric still regressed versus 1.044135489; Google-CP improved to baseline but flotilla, transfer, large-gender, and zero-regression penalty worsened.
asi.next_action_hint: The direction may merit a narrower variant only if paired with a zero-regression fix; do not keep the 250/50 oracle budget as-is.

description: Tried intermediate pure-structure oracle local search budget

asi.hypothesis: A moderate 400/80 pure-oracle budget might retain the default oracle quality while saving some construction/search wall time.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed; large-gender and Google-CP were much worse and zero-regression penalty remained above the kept line.
asi.next_action_hint: Default 500/100 oracle budget remains preferable; avoid further simple oracle-budget scalar tuning unless diagnosing a specific structural interaction.

description: Tried narrowing oracle template group-size ladder

asi.hypothesis: Removing the smallest max-2 template group-size option would avoid low-capacity oracle templates that can add dummy/displacement risk.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed; Google-CP, large-gender, flotilla, and zero-regression penalty worsened, so the max-2 option remains useful for broad balance.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the three-step group-size ladder; avoid ladder pruning without case-level telemetry showing a specific bad template size.

description: Tried extending oracle template group-size ladder

asi.hypothesis: Adding a max-3 group-size option could give the candidate scorer a lower-disruption smaller template when max/max-1/max-2 are too invasive.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed; flotilla, transfer, Google-CP, constrained 49x7, and zero-regression penalty worsened, so extra small templates add harmful choices.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the three-step max/max-1/max-2 ladder and stop ladder width tuning.

description: Tried allowing displacing oracle merge in hard-apart sessions with repair

asi.hypothesis: The hard-apart-aware displaced repair may now be strong enough to allow oracle displacement in hard-apart sessions and improve constrained basins.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed; Google-CP and zero-regression penalty worsened and flotilla did not improve, so conservative non-displacing hard-apart merge remains better.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep hard-apart sessions non-displacing; improve hard-apart cases through template selection/no-op handling rather than general displacement.

description: Tried adding evenly-spaced non-contiguous oracle template sessions

asi.hypothesis: Non-contiguous evenly-spaced session templates could improve partial-attendance or long-horizon cases that contiguous oracle windows miss.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed; transfer improved strongly, but zero-regression penalty, Google-CP, large-gender, and constrained SGP degradation outweighed it.
asi.next_action_hint: Non-contiguous templates are promising only for transfer-like partial attendance; revisit only with a selector that prevents broad cases from choosing harmful spread sessions.

description: Tried partial-attendance-gated non-contiguous oracle template sessions

asi.hypothesis: The transfer gain from non-contiguous templates could be retained by gating evenly-spaced candidates to scenarios with partial participation only.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed severely; partial-attendance gating still harmed sailing-real, flotilla, Google-CP, and constrained SGP while transfer did not improve enough.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use this coarse partial-participation gate; non-contiguous templates need much more precise telemetry/selection before retrying.

description: Tried reducing oracle target acceptance keep bonus

asi.hypothesis: Lowering the acceptance keep bonus would allow more useful oracle displacement instead of over-preserving scaffold placements.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed badly; sailing-real and Google-CP worsened, indicating keep bonus preserves important scaffold structure.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep target acceptance keep_bonus at 3.0; avoid simple acceptance scalar tuning.

description: Repeated kept constructor line without code changes after recent discards

asi.hypothesis: A further no-code repeat would calibrate whether the kept 1.044 line is reachable under current timing noise.
asi.rollback_reason: Repeat was far worse than the best observed 1.044135489 and does not replace it.
asi.next_action_hint: Noise is large, but recent no-code repeats are not beating the kept line; continue only structural changes with clear rationale.

description: Tried slightly stronger projection contact-signature weight

asi.hypothesis: A smaller increase from 0.20 to 0.25 might capture more scaffold contact signature without the over-anchoring seen at 0.30.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed badly, especially Google-CP and flotilla; 0.20 remains the best contact anchor.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop contact-signature scalar tuning; keep 0.20.

description: Tried lower high-attendance threshold for oracle template coverage

asi.hypothesis: A 2/3 high-attendance threshold might reduce dummy/omission pressure for partial-attendance templates without hard filtering.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed; zero-regression penalty, Google-CP, transfer, and flotilla worsened despite some large-gender/sailing gains.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the 3/4 high-attendance threshold; avoid attendance-threshold scalar tuning.

description: Tried selected-region assignment merge for oracle template targets

asi.hypothesis: A full selected-region assignment over mutable selected occupants plus projected candidates would be a more coherent oracle-guided fill than greedy target acceptance plus displaced repair.
asi.rollback_reason: Catastrophic quality regression without failures: pure/constrained SGP structure collapsed and zero-regression count jumped to 6, indicating the assignment fill destroyed oracle group coherence despite feasibility.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use independent selected-slot assignment. If implementing oracle-guided fill, preserve whole oracle group/cohort structure rather than assigning slots independently.

description: Tried limiting oracle imports from outside the selected merge region

asi.hypothesis: Restricting oracle imports from outside the selected region to genuine open capacity would preserve global scaffold structure while still allowing within-region oracle reshaping.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed; sailing-real became much worse and zero-regression penalty rose, so outside-region oracle imports are useful despite disruption risk.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep outside-region import behavior; structural fill changes must preserve oracle cohorts without over-constraining cross-region movement.

description: Tried projection-time attribute-balance signature scoring

asi.hypothesis: Attribute balance should influence projection before merge via a static marginal group signature instead of late repair or post-assignment swaps.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed and construction time rose sharply; attribute signature scoring worsened flotilla, Google-CP, zero-regression penalty, and constrained SGP balance.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add static approximate attribute signatures to projection; attribute-aware changes need a cheaper and more faithful group-fill model.

description: Tried adaptive longer warmup for no-repeat attribute-balance scenarios

asi.hypothesis: No-repeat attribute-balance scenarios may need slightly more full-objective warmup to avoid the zero-baseline guard without changing benchmark policy globally.
asi.rollback_reason: Two benchmark cases failed, so the adaptive 1.25s warmup exceeded strict construction budgets or otherwise destabilized the lane.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not extend warmup under the current strict budget; zero-regression fixes must come from better construction structure, not more warmup time.

description: Tried skipping no-op oracle templates with no mutable or open selected region

asi.hypothesis: Skipping templates whose selected non-hard-apart regions have no mutable/open placement capacity would avoid no-op oracle work and preserve search time.
asi.rollback_reason: Checks failed because unit tests calling oracle_template_can_change_scaffold_under_merge_policy were not updated for the new scaffold-mask argument; benchmark metric also regressed strongly.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry this no-op skip without first updating tests and only if telemetry shows such templates occur; current metric suggests it is not promising.

description: Tried single-pass constructed-schedule scoring fast path

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding the double recompute/validation path in score_constructed_schedule would reduce construction overhead and leave more effective search time without changing schedules.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed and measured construction time did not improve; noise or skipped state syncing did not translate into better search outcomes.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not keep this scoring fast path for quality; any performance work should use dedicated hotpath profiling rather than broad-suite score noise.

description: Tried pruning hard-apart conflicts from projected oracle mappings

asi.hypothesis: Structurally unmapping lower-priority projected oracle people in hard-apart co-group conflicts would prevent infeasible oracle cohorts from steering merge and improve hard-apart/flotilla basin quality.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remained worse than the true kept best 1.044135489; constrained SGP and zero-regression penalty did not improve enough despite better sailing-real.
asi.next_action_hint: Hard-apart projection pruning may be too blunt; only revisit with cohort-preserving replacement rather than dropping mapped people.

description: Tried dynamic attribute-aware oracle target acceptance

asi.hypothesis: Using exact dynamic attribute-balance marginal gain while accepting projected oracle targets would improve attribute-balanced construction basins without changing budgets or adding knobs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed above the true kept best 1.044135489; although flotilla improved, transfer/Google/Synthetic/constrained balance and runtime did not justify the change.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid simple attribute marginal terms in target acceptance; attribute handling likely needs a fuller baseline-fill redesign rather than local target ranking.

description: Tried quorum gating partial oracle target cohorts

asi.hypothesis: Accepting only projected target cohorts with at least a majority of their oracle group mapped would avoid incoherent partial oracle imports and preserve SGP/cohort structure.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed badly versus true kept best; skipping partial cohorts harmed sailing/flotilla and large immovable despite faster runtime.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use coarse quorum gating; if preserving cohorts, operate on full selected-region fill with objective-aware alternatives rather than skipping partial groups.

description: Tried mutable contact-pressure template slot selection

asi.hypothesis: Selecting equal-capacity template groups by mutable internal contact pressure would steer oracle work toward the scaffold's weakest regions and remove zero-baseline regressions without changing budgets.
asi.rollback_reason: The zero-regression guard was fixed, but the weighted successful-case mean regressed versus the true kept best, with Google/constrained/flotilla costs too high.
asi.next_action_hint: The signal is useful diagnostically; revisit only as a small term in candidate scoring or blended with disruption risk, not as a hard slot-ordering priority.

description: Tried soft mutable contact-pressure template candidate scoring

asi.hypothesis: Adding mutable internal contact pressure as a soft template-candidate score term would favor windows where oracle construction can address repeated contacts without hard-changing group slot order.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus the true kept best; soft pressure did not remove the zero-regression guard and harmed sailing/flotilla/Google balance.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue mutable-contact-pressure candidate scoring in this form; the hard-ordering variant was diagnostically interesting only for the zero guard.

description: Tried hard-apart projection swap repair

asi.hypothesis: Repairing projected hard-apart conflicts via deterministic swaps instead of unmapping would preserve oracle coverage while avoiding infeasible co-group projections.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus the true kept best and runtime increased; preserving coverage through swaps harmed sailing/Google/constrained balance.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use projection-only hard-apart swap repair; hard-apart handling likely belongs in merge/selection telemetry rather than relabeling swaps.

description: Tried contact-pressure slot ordering only for plain flexible sessions

asi.hypothesis: Using mutable contact-pressure slot ordering only in sessions without active hard-apart, cliques, or immovables would target repeat-heavy flexible regions while avoiding structural-constraint regressions.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric still regressed and the zero-regression guard remained; excluding rigid sessions was not enough to recover successful-case balance.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use contact-pressure slot ordering as a selection policy; at most collect it as telemetry for future candidate-risk diagnosis.
